title: Native AgentTeam runtime semantic architecture
summary: Define the semantic contract for a lightweight, general-purpose AgentTeam runtime that orchestrates mature agent CLIs through adapters while preserving deterministic scheduling, durable communication, authority control, and workspace isolation.
project_intent: Support long-running multi-role agent collaboration without equating logical agents with Codex subagents.
Reuse mature agent tools such as Codex, Claude Code, Aider, OpenCode, and Gemini CLI as runtime backends.
Keep AgentTeam responsible for control-plane semantics: scheduling, leases, mailbox, events, artifact authority, validation, and integration.
Remain general-purpose rather than specializing the framework only for GitHub issue-to-PR coding workflows.
confirmed_decisions: The scheduler is a deterministic software process, not an LLM role agent.
A role agent is a long-lived logical responsibility boundary, not a permanent CLI process.
A runtime session is a short-lived invocation used for one bounded attempt.
A writable attempt gets its own worktree; read-only roles do not get private worktrees by default.
MCP is used as tool and context plumbing, not as AgentTeam's internal control plane.
A2A is postponed and is not part of the M0/M1 native runtime contract.
Raw agent output is not authoritative until accepted by validation and integration gates.

included: task, attempt, lease, and worktree lifecycle
